banco_oraciones.oracion: Ben woke up late again this morning.
Kenji always looks up new words he doesn't know.
They ended up staying until midnight.
Ben didn't show up to the meeting on time.
Sofía never gives up on a difficult project.
We didn't clean up after the party.
What time did you wake up?
Why did you give up so quickly?
Did Ben show up late? Yes, he did.
Does Sofía ever give up? No, she doesn't.
Did they clean up the studio? Yes, they did.

interaccion: Sofía: You **woke up** late again, didn't you?
Ben: I didn't wake up late, I just **showed up** late. Different problem.
Kenji: Either way, we **ended up** starting the class without you.
Ben: I'll **look up** what I missed. I promise I won't **give up** on this course.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I looked up and saw the word in the sky.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: I looked up the word in the dictionary.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Phrasal verbs with 'up' rarely mean 'upward' — they usually describe completing, increasing, or discovering something.

filas: He showed up an hour late

ejercicios.pregunta: Kenji always ___ new words he doesn't know.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Look up' usually means to search for information.

ejercicios.respuesta: they ended up staying until midnight

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I looked up and saw the word in the dictionary.
ejercicios.respuesta: I looked up the word in the dictionary.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Ben arrived late to the meeting.
ejercicios.respuesta: Ben showed up late to the meeting.
Ben didn't show up on time.

ejercicios.pregunta: What time did you wake up today?

guion: Sofía: You woke up late again, I can tell.
Ben: I showed up on time, that's what matters.
Kenji: You ended up missing the first ten minutes.
Ben: I'll look up what I missed and catch up.
Sofía: Just don't give up on the whole course because of ten minutes.
Ben: Never. I'm growing up, slowly.

comprension.pregunta: What does Kenji say Ben ended up doing?
What does Ben promise not to do?

reading.texto: Kenji grew up in Osaka, in a house where everyone woke up before sunrise. In Medellín, he's slowly given up on that habit — mornings here feel different, slower. He still looks up unfamiliar words constantly, filling his notebook with phrases he hears from Ben and Sofía. Sometimes he shows up to class early just to practice with whoever's there. Other times, a good conversation with Abuela Carmen means he ends up staying for hours, notebook forgotten on the table. He hasn't given up on becoming fluent — if anything, moving here just proved how much further he still has to go, and how much he enjoys the trip.

preguntas.afirmacion: He has completely stopped waking up early since moving.
Kenji sometimes ends up staying for hours talking with Abuela Carmen.
Kenji has given up on becoming fluent.
